subject: Product Management
title: Windows Vista
description: A fast breakdown of Windows Vista for builders.

hook.text: Windows Vista took five years to build, shipped late, and was so bloated that it made brand-new computers feel slow.

buildup.text: Microsoft packed Vista with security features, visual effects, and new APIs — all running simultaneously.

discovery.text: User Account Control asked permission for everything. Users clicked 'Allow' mindlessly — defeating the security purpose.

twist.text: Hardware manufacturers shipped PCs labeled 'Vista Ready' that could barely run it. The experience was miserable on most machines.

climax.text: Businesses skipped Vista entirely. Many downgraded to Windows XP. Microsoft rushed Windows 7 as an apology.

punchline.text: More features doesn't mean a better product. Often the opposite.

quiz.question: What was Vista's core product failure?
options: Feature bloat made it slow and the security UX annoyed users into ignoring it
It had too few features compared to Windows XP
Vista was only available on Mac computers
